Shall be awarded on the basis of financial need, academic achievement and future potential.

name: Abel Family Scholarship in Physics
description: Recipients must be an undergraduate Physics major pursuing a B.S. in Physics. Eligible students must also be a sophomore level or higher, have and maintain a 3.0 cumulative GPA and a 3.0 GPA in their major, and be taking a minimum of 12 semester hours. Recipients must also work part-time, at least 10 hours per week. Paid positions at a UT Austin research lab are eligible. Previous Abel Family Scholarship recipients are eligible for renewal each semester. Students must submit the CNS Common Scholarship Application available through LASSO to be considered. The CNS Common Scholarship Application typically opens to current students in early January with a March 15 deadline. Contact scholarships@cns.utexas.edu with questions regarding the CNS Common Scholarship Application.

name: Academic Improvement Scholarship
description: The Senate of College Councils Academic Improvement Scholarship is designed to reward undergraduate students who have shown significant improvement in academics despite performing poorly in previous academic years. To be eligible, you must be a currently enrolled undergraduate student, have had a semester with a GPA lower than 2.7 and improved your GPA by at least 0.5 grade points in the coming semesters. An applicant must have made significant improvements to their GPA, and should have taken steps to ensure that they retain this academic success with the use of UT's academic resources (e.g. Sanger Learning Center, Writing Center, Counseling and Mental Health Center, etc.) Applications will be evaluated based on the quality of the short answer responses along with the GPA requirements listed above. Students who are currently serving as a member of the Senate of the College Councils are not eligible to apply. If selected to be a recipient of the Academic Improvement Scholarship, you will be ineligible to receive the award a second time.

Students in the traditional MPA program are automatically considered for a potential scholarship after gaining admission (a scholarship application is not required).

name: Aggarwal Endowed Presidential Scholarship in Indian Studies for Undergraduates
description: The Aggarwal Endowed Presidential Scholarship in Indian Studies was established by the Board of Regents of The University of Texas System on March 11, 2010, for the benefit of the College of Liberal Arts. Gift funds were provided by J.K. Aggarwal, Ph.D. of Austin, TX and Shanti J. Aggarwal, Ph.D. of Austin, TX. Award Recipient Requirements Must be a junior or senior undergraduate student Must have at least 45 hours in residence Minimum UT GPA of 3.25 Must be enrolled full time (at least 12, preferably 15 hours) in both the fall and spring semesters of the following academic year (during the tenure of the award) Strong preference in selection criteria (though not required): Commitment to about South Asian histories languages, and cultures either through current coursework, or future plans. Must be a student majoring in the College of Liberal Arts, specifically relating to Indian Studies. Award Recipient Preferences Strong preference in selection criteria (though not required): Commitment to about South Asian histories languages, and cultures either through current coursework, or future plans. Award Application Information Interested students who meet the eligibility requirements can apply for this scholarship by submitting the below materials to the South Asia Institute at sai@austin.utexas.edu : Academic Summary (from Registrar’s Office) or informal UT Transcript. One letter of recommendation from a faculty member. One-page personal statement (350 words or less) that addresses one's interest in India and future plans for the study of South Asian languages, histories, and cultures at UT and beyond. Please Note: If you are applying for the Foreign Language and Area Studies Fellowship, we will accept your two FLAS recommendation letters to apply for the Aggarwal Endowed Presidential Scholarship in Indian Studies! Award Donor Information (only required for donors receiving thank-you letters) Gift funds were provided by J.K. Aggarwal, Ph.D. of Austin, TX and Shanti J. Aggarwal, Ph.D. of Austin, TX. Applications to apply open on Wednesday, November 1, 2023. Deadline to Apply: Friday, December 1, 2023
submissionDetails: Award Application Information Interested students who meet the eligibility requirements can apply for this scholarship by submitting the below materials to the South Asia Institute at sai@austin.utexas.edu : Academic Summary (from Registrar’s Office) or informal UT Transcript. One letter of recommendation from a faculty member. Please Note: If you are applying for the Foreign Language and Area Studies Fellowship, we will accept your two FLAS recommendation letters to apply for the Aggarwal Endowed Presidential Scholarship in Indian Studies!

name: Alec Beck Endowed Scholarship
description: To award scholarships to deserving students attending UT Austin with first preference for students whose parent or step-parent is, or has been, incarcerated in or on parole from The Texas Department of Criminal Justice. Once the first preference has been fulfilled, it is permissible to give preference to students who have a parent or step parent incarcerated in or on parole from any incarceration facility or system, including the Federal System. Once need has been met for the first two preferences, it is permissible to provide scholarships to students with financial need. In all cases, if more than one student applies any semester, financial need should also be a consideration, with preference given to qualifying students with the greatest needs. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. Current UT undergraduates and incoming external transfer undergraduates can apply through the OSFA Continuing & Transfer Student Scholarship Application, available from November through mid-March each year. All entering first-time undergraduate students are encouraged to submit the Scholarship Interest Form to be considered for the maximum number of scholarships from the Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid. The Scholarship Interest Form is available in LASSO each year from September 1 - January 15.

name: Annie Whittenburg Walker Memorial Endowed Presidential Scholarship
description: Annual scholarship in the College of Business Administration to students who are seeking BBA degrees from UT Austin. Recipients must be enrolled full-time in the McCombs School of Business during the semester nominated for the scholarship and during the two semesters the student holds the scholarship, unless in final semester before graduating and a full course load is not needed. Applicants must have a cumulative GPA of at least 3.6 on the work taken in residence at UT Austin In addition to academic excellence, the Selection Committee shall equally consider independent scholastic activities, extracurricular activities, and leadership. Financial aid may be a factor in evaluating otherwise equal qualifications. Renewal of recipient shall be allowed when the student maintains the above described criteria.

name: Benjamin A. Gilman International Scholarship Program
description: The Gilman Scholarship Program supports undergraduate students who might not otherwise participate due to financial constraints. The program encourages students to study and intern in diverse world regions and to study languages, especially critical need languages. Veterans of military service are encouraged to apply, and preference is given to veterans when other factors are equivalent. The Gilman program has successfully supported students who have been historically underrepresented in education abroad, including but not limited to first-generation college students, students in STEM fields, and students with disabilities. The Gilman Scholarship Program provides additional awards for critical language study, STEM involvement, and study in countries that have additional funding opportunities. Award recipients are chosen by a competitive selection process and must use the award to defray eligible study or intern abroad costs. These costs include program tuition, room and board, books, local transportation, insurance, international airfare, passport and visa fees. For application information visit their website.

name: Betty Louise Vesowate Endowed Scholarship
description: Funds distributed from the endowment shall be used to provide scholarship support to undergraduate students in the Steve Hicks School of Social Work, with first preference given to students from a single parent home and second preference given to students studying behavioral health issues. If you are an applicant or current social work student and would like to be considered for this or other scholarships administered by the UT School of Social Work, please complete the appropriate scholarship application (BSW or MSSW). The 2026–2027 BSW Scholarship Application will open in February—don’t miss it! Watch your inbox for an email from the BSW Program with a direct link to apply. The MSSW application will open December 1, 2025, and close February 1, 2026. Please find the MSSW application here. Deadline for Merit-Based Scholarships: January 5, 2026 Deadline for Need-Based and Professional Interest Scholarships: February 1, 2026 If you are a prospective Social Work PhD student, please contact Graduate Program Coordinator Abbey Morales at ssw-doctoral@austin.utexas.edu with questions regarding the PhD scholarship awarding process.
submissionDetails: If you are an applicant or current social work student and would like to be considered for this or other scholarships administered by the UT School of Social Work, please complete the appropriate scholarship application (BSW or MSSW). The 2026–2027 BSW Scholarship Application will open in February—don’t miss it! Watch your inbox for an email from the BSW Program with a direct link to apply.

name: Betty Osborn Biedenharn Endowed Presidential Scholarship in Music
description: To support outstanding piano students in the Butler School of Music. To be awarded this Endowed Presidential Scholarship, students must be enrolled full-time in consecutive long sessions during the award period. Financial need may be considered. [Undergraduates Only] Undergraduate recipients must be a junior or senior classification with a minimum 3.25 GPA and at least 45 credit hours in residence at the university. [Graduate Students only] Graduate student recipients must hold at least one baccalaureate degree. Scholastic, artistic, musical and creative merit will be considered alongside leadership in university or industry settings College of Fine Arts Scholarship Information All students admitted to the College of Fine Arts are automatically considered for available scholarship awards. No application is required. To learn more about scholarships for continuing students, visit the College of Fine Arts scholarships webpage.
submissionDetails: Scholastic, artistic, musical and creative merit will be considered alongside leadership in university or industry settings College of Fine Arts Scholarship Information All students admitted to the College of Fine Arts are automatically considered for available scholarship awards. name: Bob and Lupe Egenolf Endowed Scholarship
description: To benefit students enrolled in the Canfield Business Honors Program in the College of Business Administration. A committee in the College of Business Administration shall select the recipient based on the suggested criteria: The selected student must be enrolled full-time in the Canfield Business Honors Program in the College of Business Administration, except that less than a full course load is permitted in a student's final semester prior to graduation if such a credit load will enable the student to complete degree requirements. Preference should be given to a freshman; however, any Canfield Business Honors student who meets the other criteria may be considered for this scholarship. Must have demonstrated academic achievement in the Canfield Business Honors Program by maintaining a minimum GPA of 3.5. If the student is a freshman, he or she must have demonstrated academic merit. Preference shall be given to a student in need of financial assistance. The scholarship may be awarded to the same student in successive semesters provided that the student maintains the above mentioned scholarship criteria.
submissionDetails: Apply through the official scholarship link for complete submission instructions.

name: Bob Brister Memorial Scholarship
description: The goal of this endowment is to provide meaningful work experiences for aspiring outdoor journalists. Recipients will be selected annually to receive a stipend to help cover living expenses associated with an unpaid 10-week summer internship at an outdoor magazine or other publication. After the satisfactory completion of the internship, the recipient will receive a scholarship for the fall semester. The size and number of awards are to be determined by the College. Criteria for the scholarship are as follows: The scholarship is open to juniors, seniors and graduate students in the professional Masters track who intend to pursue a career in outdoor writing or related areas. The recipient must be enrolled full-time in the School of Journalism at UT Austin at the time of the award. Students will submit an application with an essay explaining why they wish to pursue a career in outdoor writing and why they believe this type of writing is important.
submissionDetails: Students will submit an application with an essay explaining why they wish to pursue a career in outdoor writing and why they believe this type of writing is important.

name: Brian T. Muecke Endowed Presidential Scholarship
description: Recipients shall be students studying in one of the following colleges/departments: McCombs School of Business, Cockrell School of Engineering, Economics Department in the College of Liberal Arts, or College of Natural Sciences. First preference given to students from Houston admitted directly after completing high school (i.e., not transfer students from other institutions of higher education or UT System components) and who were NOT granted automatic admission (i.e., from outside the top percentage in effect at the time of admission). Should no student(s) fit first preference, second preference is for students from Texas who were admitted directly after completing high school (i.e., not transfer students from other institutions of higher education or UT System components) and who were NOT granted automatic admission.(i.e., from outside the top percentage in effect at the time of admission). Recipients must be juniors or seniors with at least a 3.75 GPA, 45 credit hours in residence at UT Austin, and must be enrolled in consecutive long sessions and full-time status during award period; leadership roles should be a consideration; financial need should be a consideration when selecting between equally meritorious candidates. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. This scholarship is available through the OSFA Endowed Presidential Scholarship Application, available to eligible students in LASSO each spring semester.

name: Bruce McMillan, Jr. Foundation Scholarship
description: Preference for incoming freshmen and must demonstrate financial need as exhibited in a completed FAFSA. Students must be "dependent students" as defined by the FAFSA. Incoming freshmen must have graduated in either the top fifteen percent (15%) or the top five (5) positions of their high school graduating classes (whichever group is larger). Students must be graduates of one of the eight high schools in the immediate Overton, Texas area (i.e. Arp, Carlisle, Henderson, Kilgore, Leverett's Chapel, Overton, Troup, or West Rusk). If home-schooled, the student must reside in one of those school districts: Kilgore, Arp, Carlisle, Henderson, Leveretts Chapel, Overton, Troup, West Rusk. Students are eligible for renewal if they maintain a 2.5 GPA while completing a minimum of twenty-four (24) hours of solid academic course work during the academic year (fall and spring semesters). Eligibility expires after four (4) years from high school graduation. Must not have a criminal record or otherwise be in trouble with the law. Must not receive athletic scholarships through the institution

name: Camille D. Miller Endowed Scholarship in Social Work
description: Funds distributed from the endowment shall be used to provide scholarships to undergraduates in the Steve Hicks School of Social Work. Examples include, but are not limited to, city government, state legislature, congress, state agencies, federal agencies, think tanks, and any other institutions that impact policy. If you are an applicant or current social work student and would like to be considered for this or other scholarships administered by the UT School of Social Work, please complete the appropriate scholarship application (BSW or MSSW). The 2026–2027 BSW Scholarship Application will open in February—don’t miss it! Watch your inbox for an email from the BSW Program with a direct link to apply. The MSSW application will open December 1, 2025, and close February 1, 2026. Please find the MSSW application here. Deadline for Merit-Based Scholarships: January 5, 2026 Deadline for Need-Based and Professional Interest Scholarships: February 1, 2026 If you are a prospective Social Work PhD student, please contact Graduate Program Coordinator Abbey Morales at ssw-doctoral@austin.utexas.edu with questions regarding the PhD scholarship awarding process.

name: Carlos and Clara Quintanilla Scholarship
description: The Carlos and Clara Quintanilla Scholarship is a $5,000 spring tuition award that recognizes an undergraduate international student enrolled in the Cockrell School of Engineering who will return to Mexico and positively impact the Mexican economy. Applicants must have been a resident of Mexico for at least 10 years prior to attending UT and demonstrate financial need. Application Details In addition to the online application, the following criteria must be met: 1. You must be a student currently enrolled in the Cockrell School of Engineering. 2. You resided in Mexico 10 years immediately prior to enrolling in the Cockrell School of Engineering. 3. You must be on a non-immigrant visa (F-1, J-1, etc.) and be classified as an international student at UT Austin. 4. You must be enrolled full-time at UT Austin during the award semester. 5. You must be in good academic standing with UT Austin. 6. You can demonstrate financial aid. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee receiving an award. This scholarship is administered and offered by Texas Global. International students are eligible to apply to a variety of scholarships across the University. We encourage students to connect with their advisor and college/school to learn about other opportunities. Also, please note that Texas Global scholarships are one-time awards, and recipients are not guaranteed to receive the scholarship in subsequent years. To apply for this award, log in to LASSO or click the "Apply Now" button at the top of the Scholarships Search page. Find the Carlos and Clara Quintanilla Scholarship application in the list of scholarships and click "Start Application" to the right of the page. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding.
submissionDetails: Application Details In addition to the online application, the following criteria must be met: 1. name: Carole L. Zoom Scholarship for Physically Disabled Students
description: (1) Scholarship shall be awarded to a physically disabled student. Defined as one who is mobility, hearing, or visually impaired. Priority shall be given to a mobility impaired student. If the scholarship is not awarded, the income shall be reinvested in the corpus; (2) Applicants should have a GPA of 2.5 on a 4.0 scale and be registered for six or more hours during the semesters to be covered by the scholarship; (3) The scholarship is intended to cover college related, disability related, and/or living expenses; (4) Financial need shall be only one determinant in the selection of the recipient; (5) The recipient shall receive one-half of the award at the beginning of each long semester of the academic year. Past recipients are encourage to reapply and as long as the recipient shows progress toward a degree by maintaining the stated scholastic and enrollment requirements, previous recipients shall receive preference. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. This scholarship is awarded through the OSFA Continuing & Transfer Student Scholarship Application, available to current UT undergraduates and incoming external transfer undergraduates from November through mid-March each year.

description: Charlie Haas Family Endowed Presidential Scholarship will provide unrestricted scholarships. Recipients should be considered of any classification from entering freshmen to seniors. Preference given to students of any academic major who have graduated from Corpus Christi high schools. Preference given to physically handicapped students. In a year that there may be no handicapped students, the award should be given to non-handicapped students meeting the requirements. Preference given to students with financial need. GPA for physically handicapped students should be no less than 2.5 with preference for higher academic achievement, and they should be enrolled with a minimum of 9 credit hours. GPA for non-handicapped students should be no less than a 3.5, and they should be enrolled with a minimum of 12 credit hours. Recipients must be juniors or seniors with 45 credit hours in residence at UT Austin, and must be enrolled in consecutive long sessions and full-time status during award period; leadership roles should be a consideration. name: COLA - Liberal Arts Undergraduate Research Award
description: Award provides up to $1,000 in support for specific scholarly research projects conducted by full-time Liberal Arts undergraduate students. These awards are intended to cover costs associated with academic research projects proposed and written by student applicants and undertaken with the supervision of a university tenured, tenure-track, or professional-track faculty member or full-time research staff. FALL 2025 APPLICATION DEADLINE - 10/1/25 at 11:59pm SPRING 2026 APPLICATION DEADLINE - 3/1/26 at 11:59pm Eligibility: For currently enrolled Liberal Arts undergraduates. Applicants must be enrolled full-time (minimum of 12 hours) and have a minimum in-residence GPA of 3.0. Liberal Arts Undergraduate Research Awards are given once per student, per academic year, Sept. 1–Aug. 31. A student may re-apply after an unsuccessful application. Award limit is $1,000 per project. ​​​​​​​Research projects must be independent, i.e., the student has an intellectual stake in the project rather than solely assisting with a professor’s project. Research supervisors must be affiliated with UT Austin as tenured, tenure-track, or professional-track faculty members, or full-time research staff. The supervisor endorse the applicant via letter of recommendation, and will attest to the appropriateness of the student’s research expenses throughout the project and will need Principal Investigator (PI) status in order to request IACUC, IBC, or IRB approvals if applicable. ​​​​​​​Travel costs (outside Austin), including conference travel, are only eligible if expense can be justified as contributing to the Liberal Arts Undergraduate Research Award goals and objectives. Please email cola-scholarships@austin.utexas.edu with questions. ​​​​​​​
submissionDetails: FALL 2025 APPLICATION DEADLINE - 10/1/25 at 11:59pm SPRING 2026 APPLICATION DEADLINE - 3/1/26 at 11:59pm Eligibility: For currently enrolled Liberal Arts undergraduates. A student may re-apply after an unsuccessful application. The supervisor endorse the applicant via letter of recommendation, and will attest to the appropriateness of the student’s research expenses throughout the project and will need Principal Investigator (PI) status in order to request IACUC, IBC, or IRB approvals if applicable.

description: 1) U.S. citizens and permanent residents must be given equal consideration. 2) Shall have completed all high school work in either Texas or Nebraska. 3) Shall have attained the rank of sophomore as defined by UT Austin. 4) Shall be carrying twelve (12) semester hours or more of work. 5) Shall be a serious, purposeful scholar maintaining a scholastic average of 3.50 or better. 6) Shall not have received benefits in any amount from this Fund for a total of more than twelve (12) semesters. 7) Shall major in Business Administration, Engineering, Chemistry, Computer Science, Geological Science, Mathematics, Physics. 8) Shall have documented financial need. name: CREEES Czech Studies Scholarship
description: Description: The Center for Russia, East Europe and Eurasian Studies is offering competitive scholarships of up to $5,000 for intensive Czech language programs. Programs that do not primarily focus on Czech language learning are eligible for awards of up to $1,000. These funds must be used to cover travel, housing, tuition or other related costs for studying Czech.. Funded by endowments for Czech Studies at The University of Texas at Austin, supported by the Czech Educational Foundation of Texas along with various cultural organizations and individual donors. (maximum award of $5,000) Requires 250 word essay outlining objectives and activities relating to language or area studies. Eligibility: UT Austin students of any major Acceptance into Czech language program Completed application at least 6 weeks prior to start of program (essay, proof of acceptance, program details) For questions please reach out to scholarships coordinator catherine.forrest@austin.utexas.edu or creees@austin.utexas.edu
submissionDetails: (maximum award of $5,000) Requires 250 word essay outlining objectives and activities relating to language or area studies. name: CREEES McWilliams Scholarship
description: Description: The Center for Russia, East Europe and Eurasian Studies offers competitive scholarships ranging from $500-1,500 to be used for the study of Russian language and culture, professional development or research related to Russia or the former Soviet Union in any field. These funds must be used to cover tuition, travel, housing or other related costs relevant to the study of Russia or the former Soviet Union. Funds may be used for: Summer study of Russian language (summer programs must be at least 5 weeks) Conducting research in Russia/the former Soviet Union (with an explanation of your research project, objectives, sources, etc) Professional development activities (internship, presenting at a conference, job interview, etc) Eligibility: undergraduate or graduate status in any discipline at UT-Austin and in good academic standing; may NOT be combined with other significant grant/scholarship funds (ie. can not also be a FLAS or Fulbright-Hays grant recipient); must agree to write a statement to be published in area newsletters and online describing their experience in Russia/former Soviet Union;

name: CREEES Polish Studies Scholarship
description: Description: The Center for Russia, East Europe and Eurasian Studies is offering competitive scholarships to be used for the study of Polish language and culture, or research related to Poland in any field. Travel may take place for summer or during the academic year. These funds must be used to cover travel, housing, tuition or other related costs while studying the Polish language domestically or abroad, conducting research, or completing professional internships related to Polish Studies (maximum award amount of $5,000): Studying Polish language domestically or abroad (summer programs must be at least 3 weeks) - See a list of UT Study Abroad programs in Poland here! Conducting research (with an explanation of your research project, objectives, sources, etc.) The Culture.pl internship in Warsaw at the Adam Mickiewicz Institute Funded by the Polish Studies Endowment at The University of Texas at Austin and made possible by the Austin Polish Society and various individual donors. Award recipients must agree to do a short presentation about their study/research experience in Poland for the Austin Polish Society (APS) Board of Directors upon return to Austin. Eligibility: UT Austin students of any major in good standing May not be combined with other significant scholarships (FLAS, Fullbright) Completed application at least 6 weeks prior to start of program (essay, descripton of study/research plan/program) For questions please reach out to scholarships coordinator catherine.forrest@austin.utexas.edu or creees@austin.utexas.edu
submissionDetails: Eligibility: UT Austin students of any major in good standing May not be combined with other significant scholarships (FLAS, Fullbright) Completed application at least 6 weeks prior to start of program (essay, descripton of study/research plan/program) For questions please reach out to scholarships coordinator catherine.forrest@austin.utexas.edu or creees@austin.utexas.edu

name: CREEES Professional Development Scholarship
description: Scholarship Description: These awards serve to promote professional activities, language studies, and other activities and avenues related to the REEE region (Russia, East Europe and/or the former Soviet Union). A funding request that gets approval could be funded by one or more of the available scholarships funds from CREEES (Center for Russian, East European and Eurasian Studies). Eligibility: UT Austin Undergraduate and Graduate students of any major Any activity for which you seek funding MUST be related to the REEE region. To be considered, you MUST upload any/all required proof of your activity. Request types and required materials: Presenting at a conference - abstract and evidence of acceptance. Interning or applying for a job - an offer/invitation letter. Conducting field work or research - invitation/acceptance letter from institution abroad or verification statement from your supervisor. Review and Funding Details: Requests are reviewed on a rolling (monthly) basis. Please submit your request as early as possible as this will increase the likelihood of your request being approved due to limited annual funding. Priority deadlines are November 1st for Academic Year requests and April 1st for Summer Requests Please note that if your request is approved, the amount you requested will be dependent on the funding source and may not be equal to the amount we are able to offer. If applicable, this will be clearly noted in your notification email. Domestic: Up to $750 Interational: Up to $1250 Awardee Requirements: Students awarded funds from CREEES, will be asked to submit a paragraph or short video/audio recording about the experience with at least one picture of yourself, to post online and disseminate through our website, social media, emails to donors/funding sources, and our e-newsletter. This also serves to justify our investment in our various sources of funding. By submitting this form, you acknowledge that you will help provide information on your experience to our staff in a timely manner after the completion of your proposed objective or activity. Please reach out to catherine.forrest@austin.utexas.edu with questions.
submissionDetails: Scholarship Description: These awards serve to promote professional activities, language studies, and other activities and avenues related to the REEE region (Russia, East Europe and/or the former Soviet Union). Interning or applying for a job - an offer/invitation letter. Please submit your request as early as possible as this will increase the likelihood of your request being approved due to limited annual funding.

name: Dean's Graduate Fellowship
description: *For Current LBJ School Master's Students* The Dean's Graduate Fellowship is awarded annually on a competitive basis to degree-seeking second-year master's students who have demonstrated excellence in their first year of study. name: Department of Asian Studies Scholarships
description: There are many opportunities for students to apply for scholarships, both within and outside the department/college. Listed below are scholarships available throughout the school year: students should start their planning well in advance so as not to miss any deadlines. Many of these scholarships are for study abroad, but several can be used for general expenses. Since many scholarships are need-based, you will want to ensure your Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) is on file at UT. The FAFSA is available after January 1; students should submit their application as early as possible. Several of these scholarships are accessed through the GLOBAL A$$IST website. Please apply only to the scholarship that corresponds to the semester in which you will begin your study or research program.

name: Department of Defense (DoD) SMART Scholarship-for-Service Program
description: SMART is a scholarship-for-service program established by the Department of Defense (DoD) to enhance the workforce with qualified civilian scientists, researchers, and engineers. SMART offers students pursuing STEM degrees full tuition, an annual stipend, book and health insurance allowances, summer internships, and guaranteed civilian employment with the DoD upon graduation. Scholars work as scientists, engineers, or researchers at one of over 150 innovative laboratories across the Army, Navy, Air Force, and larger DoD within the United States.

name: Dieste Scholarship for Innovation and Creativity
description: Criteria: -Minimum GPA of 3.0 -At least 45 hours taken in residence at UT (by Fall) -Undergraduate major in Advertising or Public Relations -Demonstrates creativity and innovation in problem-solving. To be considered for this award, please provide an example of when you demonstrated creativity and innovation in problem solving. Examples may include, but are not limited to, coming up with a distinctive solution to a problem faced by the University, community, or family; students who have solved an artistic, scientific, or technical problem in new or unusual ways; students who have developed an innovative way to save the environment or improve people's health; or students who have developed a new mobile solution or application that provides consumer or business utility. (7,500 character max)
submissionDetails: Examples may include, but are not limited to, coming up with a distinctive solution to a problem faced by the University, community, or family; students who have solved an artistic, scientific, or technical problem in new or unusual ways; students who have developed an innovative way to save the environment or improve people's health; or students who have developed a new mobile solution or application that provides consumer or business utility.

name: District Rotary Peace Fellowship
description: Each year, Rotary awards up to 130 fully funded fellowships for dedicated leaders from around the world to study at a Rotary Foundation peace center. Through academic training, practice, and global networking opportunities, the Rotary Peace Centers program develops the capacity of peace and development professionals to become effective catalysts for peace. The fellowships cover tuition and fees, room and board, round-trip transportation, and internship and field-study expenses.

Students in the integrated MPA and MPA Bridge programs will receive an invitation to apply for scholarships before entering graduate school. Students in the traditional MPA program are automatically considered for a potential scholarship after gaining admission (a scholarship application is not required).

description: Scholarship support for non-traditional students who plan to attend medical school or go into medicine. name: Dr. David Nilsson Scholarship
description: The Dr. David Nilsson Scholarship provides a $2,000 tuition award for the spring semester to an undergraduate student who has graduated from a high school in Mexico and demonstrates financial need. In addition to the online application, the following criteria must be met: You must be registered as a full-time student at UT Austin during the semester in which the scholarship is awarded. Admitted to UT Austin as an undergraduate student. Graduated from a high school in Mexico. Be able to demonstrate financial need. Maintain a minimum 3.0 GPA for the duration of the award. Priority is given to students who: Graduated from a high school in Tejupilco or Querétaro. Are not eligible to receive a waiver of the nonresident portion of tuition. Are not eligible to fill out FAFSA or receive state or federal financial aid. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee receiving an award.

name: Education Abroad Students: International Education Fee Scholarship (IEFS) for Graduate Students - Fall 2026
description: The International Education Fee Scholarship (IEFS) was initially established in 1989 by a student referendum to encourage international education, including support for education abroad and international students. This scholarship supports enrolled UT Austin students studying, interning, or conducting research outside the U.S. Academic merit, financial need, and program factors are considered. Award amounts range between $1,200 - $1,800. Applications for graduate students going abroad in Fall 2026 are now being accepted. To apply for this award, log in to LASSO or click the "Apply Now" button at the top of the Scholarships Search page. Find the Education Abroad: International Education Fee Scholarship (IEFS) for Graduate Students - Fall 2026 application in the list of scholarships and click "Start Application" to the right of the page. Note: This application is specifically for the Fall 2026 semester and for graduate students. If you are an undergraduate student, DO NOT COMPLETE THIS APPLICATION. Complete the application titled "Education Abroad: International Education Fee Scholarship (IEFS) for Undergraduate Students - Fall 2026" if you are an undergraduate student. Graduate students are eligible to apply for education abroad in any international location, on any program, and any length of time. Students should be prepared to answer essay questions and questions regarding their program. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee receiving an award.

name: El Paso Community Endowed Dean's Scholarship
description: Funds distributed from the endowment shall be administered under the guidelines of the University's Endowed Presidential Scholarship Program to provide scholarship support for undergraduate students in the McCombs School of Business. Recipients may be incoming freshmen. Recipients must be enrolled full-time. Exceptions to this requirement may be made for students in their final semester prior to graduation if a less-than-full-time load will enable them to complete degree requirements. Recipients must maintain a minimum 3.25 grade point average. The scholarship may be awarded to the same student in successive years provided the student satisfies the above minimum criteria and reapplies. Recipients must have lived in the Paso del Norte Region or have graduated from a high school in the region, defined by the greater El Paso/Las Cruces/Juarez area, including in and around El Paso County, TX; Dona Ana County, New Mexico; Otero County, New Mexico; and Ciudad Juarez,Chihuahua Mexico.
submissionDetails: Apply through the official scholarship link for complete submission instructions.

name: Eric M. Suhl Endowed Scholarship
description: The Eric M. Suhl Scholarship was established in 2019 with funding provided by the Eric M. Suhl Foundation (www.ericmsuhlfoundation.com). The Foundation was formed to honor Eric, who passed away in 2016 at the age of 28. He lived with colitis for many years. This scholarship is dedicated to the memory of Eric and is directed to help UT students who live with colitis, Crohn’s, IBS, or any chronic medical issue. At least one scholarship for a minimum of $1,000 will be awarded for use during the academic year. If funding is available, additional scholarships and/or scholarships of a larger monetary value will be awarded. Applications are accepted during the spring semester and are awarded in the fall semester.

name: Ethel Loving de Diaz Scholarship
description: The Ethel Loving de Diaz Scholarship is a $4,500 spring tuition award that recognizes an undergraduate student who lived, studied, or traveled extensively in Mexico who is working to help fund their education. This award is intended for students with financial need who can demonstrate how their work experience has helped finance their education. Students who are the first member of their families to attend university are especially encouraged to apply. In addition to the online application, the following criteria must be met: You must be registered as a full-time student at UT Austin during the semester in which the scholarship is awarded. Completed part or all of their preparatory education Mexico. Enrolled at the time of application and in the semester the award is granted. Maintain satisfactory academic progress (2.0 GPA - undergraduate; 3.0 GPA - graduate). Demonstrate financial need. Demonstrate how work experience has helped you fund your education. Not be considered a Texas Resident for tuition purposes. (Exception: those who receive resident tuition due to an academic assignment or university scholarship/fellowship are eligible to apply). Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee receiving an award.

name: ETS Lenora M. Green Scholarship
description: This national scholarship opportunity is designed to support any full-time freshman, sophomore or junior at a public or private four-year U.S. college or university. The scholarship award is $20,000 and the application opens February 1, 2024 and the deadline is March 15, 2024. The scholarship is named in honor of the late Lenora M. Green, former Executive Director of the Center for Advocacy & Philanthropy at ETS. She focused CAAP’s mission on using social investment and advocacy programs to mitigate challenges in higher education faced by disadvantaged groups by identifying, supporting, and helping national, regional, and local organizations that advance quality and equity in education. Who Is Eligible to Apply? Any student who: is enrolled full-time as a freshman, sophomore or junior at a public or private four-year U.S. college or university has a current GPA of 3.5 or higher demonstrates leadership abilities and commitment to community service demonstrates financial need is a US citizen or legal permanent resident with a valid permanent resident card or passport stamped I-551 How to apply: To apply for the scholarship you must provide: a one-page statement outlining your financial need an official transcript three recommendation letters two essays (see application for additional details) Application open February 1 to March 15. Apply here: https://webportalapp.com/sp/login/ets_lenoragreen_scholarship
submissionDetails: The scholarship award is $20,000 and the application opens February 1, 2024 and the deadline is March 15, 2024. Green, former Executive Director of the Center for Advocacy & Philanthropy at ETS. Who Is Eligible to Apply?

name: Finis Welch Foundation Scholarship
description: The sole benefactor, Finis Welch, established the Finis Welch Foundation in 2012 to provide college scholarships to high achieving Texas students who can demonstrate financial need. The Foundation reflects Finis’ lifelong commitment to education. Currently the Finis Welch Foundation works with partnering universities to help locate and select Welch Scholars. The scholarship is a merit based, four-year scholarship for Texas residents that are selected due to their outstanding academic achievement and the potential they demonstrate. The Foundation is a last dollar scholarship provider that not only covers tuition, but also living and school expenses so that each Scholar can concentrate on their studies. Along with providing life-changing financial support, the Finis Welch Foundation enhances the educational experience with additional life skill resources, including job shadow opportunities, advising services, information seminars and much more. Requirements: To be considered for the Finis Welch Foundation Scholarship at The University of Texas at Austin, applicants must complete the following: Complete your application for admission. Complete the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA). The FAFSA application will determine your eligibility for federal grants and is required for all candidates and finalists.

name: First Abroad Scholarship
description: First Abroad Scholarship Application The First Abroad Scholarship supports first-generation students planning for a study abroad experience. It is a one-time scholarship of $3,000 used to fund participation in a UT- approved study abroad program of at least 3 weeks. This scholarship is ideal for first-generation students that know they want to study abroad but have not yet chosen a program. Students who are eligible for this award must meet the following criteria: You are a first-generation college student You are an undergraduate student Your first semester at UT will be Fall 2025 You have a current FAFSA on file with UT Austin You did not transfer from a four year university Awards are intended to be used during the standard four years of an undergraduate education. Awards from the 2025 fall application cycle will be announced in mid to late January 2026. Scholarship notifications will be made via email. Scholarship awards from the 2025 fall application cycle will be available for use starting Fall 2026. These awards are considered planning scholarships and are not intended for Spring programs, including May terms, immediately following the award announcements (Spring 2026, May 2026).
submissionDetails: First Abroad Scholarship Application The First Abroad Scholarship supports first-generation students planning for a study abroad experience. name: Florence Ralston Brooke Austin High School Athletic Cup and Scholarship Fund
description: Florence Ralston Brooke Austin High School Athletic Cup and Scholarship Fund will be awarded to an Austin High School student who lettered in football, basketball, baseball or track; nominated by the principal. Candidate must have received three years continuous instruction in the Austin High School. No failing grades of any kind shall be made in these consecutive years of high school. A high order of scholastic attainment shall be made; that is, a grade average of not less than a straight C average, not less than seventy-five percent average on any work done in any course, and a total average in not less than a B of not less than eighty-six percent on the entire work required for this award. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. All entering first-time undergraduate students are encouraged to submit the Scholarship Interest Form to be considered for the maximum number of scholarships from the Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid. The Scholarship Interest Form is available in LASSO each year from September 1 - January 15.

name: Freeman Scholars Internship Stipend (Spring 2026)
description: Freeman Scholars Internship Stipend Application This scholarship provides up to $6,500.00 in scholarships for students completing an internship in East or Southeast Asia. A project is required upon completion of the internship. Eligibility requirements: Student must be completing an internship of at least 6 weeks in one of the following locations: Cambodia, Indonesia, Japan, Laos, Macao, Malaysia, Mongolia, Singapore, South Korea, Taiwan, Thailand, or Vietnam. The internship may not be a virtual/remote internship. Must be a U.S. citizen or permanent resident. Must be enrolled at the University of Texas at Austin as a degree-seeking undergraduate or graduate student. Must be in good academic and disciplinary standing throughout the application process and internship. Students should be prepared to answer essay questions and questions regarding their program. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee receiving an award. More information regarding the Freeman scholarship can be found on our website. Applications will still be accepted after the priority deadline. Questions regarding this scholarship can be directed to txglobal-scholarships@austin.utexas.edu or txglobal-freeman@austin.utexas.edu.

name: General Financial Aid Scholarship for International Students - Spring/Summer 2026
description: The General Financial Aid Scholarship provides $4,000 for the spring 2026 semester and $2,500 for the summer 2026 semester for students who demonstrate financial need. This is a competitive process as the number of awards allotted per semester is limited. Only students who have completed at least 2 semesters at UT Austin by the time the award is disbursed are eligible to apply for this award. This award does not provide a waiver of the nonresident portion of a student’s tuition & fee bill. Eligibility Requirements You must have completed a full course of study for two academic semesters at UT Austin (Concurrent enrollment at another school does not count toward full-time hours). Your GPA must be at least 2.0 (undergraduate student) or 3.0 (graduate student). You must demonstrate financial need. You must be enrolled full-time at UT Austin during the period that the financial aid is awarded. Priority is given to students who: Are not eligible to receive a waiver of the nonresident portion of tuition. Can document a change in their financial situation since initial enrollment. The General ISSS Financial Aid Scholarship is only for formula-funded courses (Option I) and Option III students are not eligible for the award. Additional information about Option III can be found here. To apply for this award, log in to LASSO or click the "Apply Now" button at the top of the Scholarships Search page. Find the General Financial Aid Scholarship for International Students - Spring/Summer 2026 application in the list of scholarships and click "Start Application" to the right of the page. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding.
submissionDetails: Only students who have completed at least 2 semesters at UT Austin by the time the award is disbursed are eligible to apply for this award. name: George L. Clark Scholarship
description: Recipients to be Texas residents with strong academic record, leadership qualities, good character and community service. Generally, recipients should rank in the top 10% of their high school graduating class. Awards may also be made to students already enrolled in The University and may be continued for graduate and professional studies, at the discretion of the Committee. Special consideration will be given to the following persons and their dependents: employees, retired employees, and directors of MCorp and its affiliates, or any successor corporations. Special consideration will also be given to residents of the marketing area of MCorp and its affiliates, or any successor corporations (now JPMorgan Chase). Awards are not limited, however, to persons in these categories. Scholarship will be administered by the Office of Students Financial Aid at UT. Recipients with demonstrable financial need may receive awards in excess of the minimum amount, as determined by the Office of Student Financial Aid. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. Current UT undergraduates and incoming external transfer undergraduates can apply through the OSFA Continuing & Transfer Student Scholarship Application, available from November through mid-March each year. All entering first-time undergraduate students are encouraged to submit the Scholarship Interest Form to be considered for the maximum number of scholarships from the Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid. The Scholarship Interest Form is available in LASSO each year from September 1 - January 15.
submissionDetails: Current UT undergraduates and incoming external transfer undergraduates can apply through the OSFA Continuing & Transfer Student Scholarship Application, available from November through mid-March each year. name: Global Change Maker Scholarships (CGB)
description: International experience transforms lives by facilitating new experiences and perspectives, cultivating adaptability and resilience, expanding horizons, and preparing students to lead in a world in which business has no borders. To help make that happen, the Global Change Maker Scholarship program supports the pursuit of study abroad and international internships by undergraduate students from all majors. Awards range from $500 to $5,000 and recipients are selected based on interest in and commitment to the pursuit of a future in international business, as well as academic and leadership experience and financial need. No one factor is dispositive, and students from all backgrounds and majors are encouraged to apply. Note: Applications for Spring, May Term, and Summer 2027 travel will open in August. Please visit the program website at bit.ly/cgb-scholarships for more information and to apply, and contact the Center for Global Business at msb-cgb@mccombs.utexas.edu with any questions.

name: Graduate School - College Recruitment Fellowship
description: Applications for Graduate School fellowships are not accepted directly from students; instead, graduate programs submit nominations. Decentralized fellowships are administered by the Graduate School, but are defined—and awardees are selected—by the college/program. Graduate advisers have the vital role of identifying and nominating eligible students. These fellowships are prestigious awards funded by the Graduate School to attract top quality graduate students to the university. Students not currently or previously enrolled in the Graduate School are eligible. Candidates must rank in the top 10 percent of students in their discipline, and most disciplines are eligible. These fellowships might be full fellowships or one-time-payment fellowships.

name: Graduate School - Harrington Recruitment Fellowship
description: Applications for Graduate School fellowships are not accepted directly from students; instead, graduate programs submit nominations. Consult with the graduate program to which you are applying for nomination information. The Donald D. Harrington Graduate Fellows Program was established by Sybil Harrington as a tribute to her late husband. She envisioned a program that would support gifted and ambitious young scholars at the university. These talented students would, in turn, share their knowledge and success with future generations. Fellowships, including a stipend of $45,000-$50,000 for 12 months, tuition and required fees, an allowance for student medical insurance, and $2,000 for miscellaneous expenses, are awarded annually to the most highly qualified applicants or nominees. Harrington Doctoral Fellows are chosen from among the successful external applicants to programs leading to a doctoral degree—Ph.D., Ed.D. or DMA. The fellowship is awarded for five years. Three of those years are full fellowship years. During the other two years, the program provides support for all three semesters (fall/spring/summer) that is supplemented by the Harrington Fellowship to keep the stipend level constant. The length of the Harrington Fellowship may be adjusted for doctoral programs that are less than five years per their degree plan. Funding after the first year is contingent on satisfactory academic progress. Harrington Master’s Fellows are chosen from among the successful external applicants to programs leading to terminal master’s degrees—for example, the MFA. The fellowship may be awarded on a one- or two-year basis. In the case of a two-year award, funding after the first year is contingent on satisfactory academic progress. Information is available at https://gradschool.utexas.edu/funding/fellowships/grad-school/recruitment
submissionDetails: Applications for Graduate School fellowships are not accepted directly from students; instead, graduate programs submit nominations. name: Graduate School - McNair Fellowship (Graduate Advancement Fellowship)
description: Applications for Graduate School fellowships are not accepted directly from students; instead, graduate programs submit nominations. Consult with the graduate program to which you are applying for nomination information. Graduate advancement fellowships help recruit a diverse graduate student body at The University of Texas at Austin. Consistent with the criteria set forth in the Graduate School’s admission policy, selection for these fellowships will be based on a holistic review of the student’s application file. Unless otherwise noted, each provides a $35,000, 12-month stipend, health insurance assistance and a tuition assistance benefit. The McNair Scholars Graduate School Fellowships Program was initiated by the Graduate School to encourage undergraduate McNair Scholars to pursue graduate education at the university. Available at more than 100 colleges and universities throughout the country, the McNair Scholars Program prepares undergraduate students for doctoral studies through involvement in research and other scholarly activities. Students who were McNair Scholars during their undergraduate work are eligible for nomination. More information is available at https://gradschool.utexas.edu/funding/fellowships/grad-school/recruitment

name: Graduate School - Mentoring Fellowship
description: Applications for Graduate School fellowships are not accepted directly from students; instead, graduate programs submit nominations. Consult with the graduate program to which you are applying for nomination information. The Mentoring Fellowship program is intended to assist graduate programs at The University of Texas at Austin in recruiting outstanding new graduate students for their program and the campus. Mentoring Fellowships are reserved for students who are U.S. citizens (or permanent residents) who are entering graduate school at the university for the first time. Other goals of the fellowship include: Support of students who might otherwise find it difficult to pursue graduate study; A mentoring plan between a faculty member in the program and the student who is recruited. Mentoring awards are allocated to the Colleges and Schools, and each Dean’s office will determine which programs are eligible to nominate a student/faculty mentor for this award. More information is available at https://gradschool.utexas.edu/funding/fellowships/grad-school/recruitment

name: Graduate School - NSF CSGrad4US Fellowship
description: Students must apply to the National Science Foundation for the NSF CSGrad4US Fellowship Program. The funding is then administered by the UT Graduate School. The objective of the Computer and Information Science and Engineering (CISE) Graduate Fellowships (CSGrad4US) is to increase the number of diverse, domestic graduate students pursuing research and innovation careers in the CISE fields: computer science, computer engineering, or information science. Further information can be found at https://cra.org/csgrad4us/ The National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellowship Program offers five-year graduate fellowships (including three years of full stipend support) for students in science and engineering fields. Fellowships are intended for students who have completed no more than 12 months of full-time graduate study. Committees look for strong grade-point average and GRE scores. About 1,000 awards are offered nationwide. Eligibility is limited to U.S. citizens or nationals studying mathematical, physical, biological, engineering, or social sciences, including the history and philosophy of science. More information is available at https://www.nsf.gov/cise/CSGrad4US/

name: Graduate School - Professional Development Award
description: Applications for Graduate School fellowships are not accepted directly from students; instead, graduate programs submit nominations. Contact your Graduate Coordinator for nomination information. Professional Development Awards provide support for graduate students to attend major professional meetings at which they present an original paper based on their research conducted at the University of Texas. The Graduate School allocates travel funds to each program at the beginning of each academic year. The graduate adviser and graduate coordinator can nominate students for these awards, which are approved and administered by the Graduate School. Amounts can vary, but the recommended range is $100-1,000, with it suggested that students be limited to one award per academic year. Students must be registered full time during the semester the funds are to be used. (Registration for the prior spring semester will fulfill this requirement for the summer semester unless the student has graduated.) More information is available at https://gradschool.utexas.edu/funding/awards/travel

name: Home to Texas
description: Home to Texas™ is a scholarship-funded summer program that connects first-year students with internship and research opportunities in their hometown communities. Many students from small or rural communities come to UT Austin; however, few return to their hometowns after graduation. As a result, many Texas communities lose a significant talent base of future professionals and leaders. In response to this emigration of talent, Career Success hosts the Home to Texas program (an initiative started by IC2 Institute and School of Undergraduate Studies ). Undergraduate student participants return to their hometowns after their first year at UT Austin to complete a full-time summer internship and to engage in community research. The goals of this program are three-fold: to develop a strong professional foundation early during the student’s UT experience, to build connections for a potential future in the student’s home community, and to help prepare the next generation of community leaders.
submissionDetails: Apply through the official scholarship link for complete submission instructions.

name: Honorable Hilmar G. Moore Endowed Scholarship
description: Students graduating from high schools in Fort Bend County, including public, private, charter, and home- schools, may apply upon admission to UT Austin or as undergraduates already enrolled at the University. Financial need, leadership qualities, and evidence of community service will be considered equally in selecting recipients from the pool of those who meet the minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale for all applicants. Recipients must be U.S. citizens or permanent residents. First preference: Qualified students graduating from Lamar Consolidated Independent School District or Needville Independent School District, or graduating from a private or home school program within the boundaries of Lamar Consolidated Independent School District or Needville Independent School District. Second preference: Qualified students graduating from any other school districts within the boundaries of Fort Bend County, Texas, including Katy ISD, Fort Bend ISD, and Stafford Municipal School District, and/or graduating from a private school or home school program within the boundaries of these districts or other areas of Fort Bend County, Texas. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. All entering first-time undergraduate students are encouraged to submit the Scholarship Interest Form to be considered for the maximum number of scholarships from the Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid. The Scholarship Interest Form is available in LASSO each year from September 1 - January 15.

name: ILAS - Tinker Field Research Grant
description: Tinker Field Research Grants are for graduate students with little or no previous field experience to enable them to conduct pre-dissertation field research in Latin America or the Caribbean, including territories. Students will be able to acquire a comprehensive knowledge of language, terrain, and culture; familiarize themselves with information sources relevant to their studies; conduct pilot studies and preliminary investigations; and develop contacts with scholars and institutions in their fields. The Tinker Field Research Grants are not to be used for advanced dissertation research, but rather to provide graduate students with an introduction to, or formative experience with, hands-on field research in Latin America or the Caribbean. Eligibility: UT graduate students in any department, discipline, or professional school may apply. Only students at the master’s or early-stage doctoral levels, or those pursuing professional degrees, are eligible. All-But-Dissertation (ABD) doctoral students are not eligible. Funds are only for pre-dissertation or master’s thesis field research in any Latin American or Caribbean country or territory, with limited funds available for non-Spanish- and non-Portuguese-speaking locations. Applicants who have received the Tinker Field Research Grant once are eligible to apply. However, priority will be given to first-time applicants. If you have any questions about this scholarship, please email llilas.gra@austin.utexas.edu.

You must be in good academic standing with UT Austin. You can demonstrate financial aid. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee receiving an award.

name: James W. and Carla S. Vick Endowed Scholarship in Social Work
description: Funds distributed from the endowment shall be used to provide scholarship support according to University Leadership Network (ULN) program guidelines. The ULN shall select recipients from students enrolled in the Hicks School of Social Work who are participating in ULN. Should there be no available student in the ULN program in any given year, the Hicks School of Social Work may make an award to any student in the school with significant financial need. Should the ULN program cease to exist, distributions shall be used for scholarships for Hicks School of Social Work students in a successor or similar program that endeavors to provide financial support and activities that promote leadership skills and academic success to students who have significant financial need. Should no such program exist, the distributions shall be used to provide scholarships to Hicks School of Social Work students with financial need. If you are an applicant or current social work student and would like to be considered for this or other scholarships administered by the UT School of Social Work, please complete the appropriate scholarship application (BSW or MSSW). The 2026–2027 BSW Scholarship Application will open in February—don’t miss it! Watch your inbox for an email from the BSW Program with a direct link to apply. The MSSW application will open December 1, 2025, and close February 1, 2026. Please find the MSSW application here. Deadline for Merit-Based Scholarships: January 5, 2026 Deadline for Need-Based and Professional Interest Scholarships: February 1, 2026 If you are a prospective Social Work PhD student, please contact Graduate Program Coordinator Abbey Morales at ssw-doctoral@austin.utexas.edu with questions regarding the PhD scholarship awarding process.

name: John and Brenda Duncan Endowed Dean's Scholarship in Business
description: The John and Brenda Duncan Endowed Dean's Scholarship in Business was established by the Board of Regents of The University of Texas System on March 12, 2013, to benefit The University of Texas McCombs School of Business. Gift funds were provided by Mr. John House Duncan, Sr. and Mrs. Brenda Pritchard Duncan. Funds distributed from the endowment shall be administered under guidelines of University's Endowed Presidential Scholarship (EPS) Program to provide scholarship support for undergraduates in the McCombs School of Business. Recipients shall be selected by a faculty committee in the McCombs School of Business, with approval of the Dean or the Dean's representative. Recipients may be incoming freshmen and must be enrolled full-time in the McCombs School, unless in their final semester and a less-than-full-time course load will enable them to complete degree requirements. Recipients will be selected based on merit and must maintain grade point average recognized by the McCombs School in keeping with guidelines of the EPS Program. Scholarship may be awarded to same student in successive years provided student satisfies above minimum criteria and reapplies for scholarship. The Dean or dean's designated representative will determine size and number of awards in any year in keeping with award amounts required by EPS Program.
submissionDetails: Apply through the official scholarship link for complete submission instructions.

name: John and Catherine Early Endowed Scholarship
description: Recipients of the John and Catherine Early Endowed Scholarship should be students who are the spouse, child, grandchild, or great-grandchild of a disabled veteran of the United States Armed Services, with a service-connected disability. Consideration is also given to the spouse, child, grandchild, or great-grandchild of an honorably discharged veteran of the United States Armed Services. Applicants must be undergraduate students and maintain a GPA of 2.8. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. All entering first-time undergraduate students are encouraged to submit the Scholarship Interest Form to be considered for the maximum number of scholarships from the Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid. The Scholarship Interest Form is available in LASSO each year from September 1 - January 15.

name: John Gregg Middleton Memorial Scholarship for the Energy Management Program
description: The John Gregg Middleton Endowed Scholarship for the Energy Management Program was established by the Board of Regents of The University of Texas System on August 22, 2014 to benefit The University of Texas McCombs School of Business. The endowment honors Mr. John G. Middleton. Gift funds were provided by Mr. D. Mayes Middleton. Funds distributed from the endowment will be used in perpetuity to provide scholarship support to undergraduate students in the energy management program area in the McCombs School of Business. Recipients shall be selected by a faculty committee in the McCombs School of Business, with approval of the Dean or the Dean's designated representative. Recipients may be incoming freshmen. Recipients must be enrolled full-time in the McCombs School of Business. Exceptions to this requirement may be made for students in their final semester prior to graduation if a less-than-full-time course load will enable the students to complete their degree requirements. The Scholarship may be awarded to the same student in successive years provided that the student satisfies the above minimum criteria and reapplies for the Scholarship. The Dean or Dean's designated representative will determine the size and number of awards in any year.
submissionDetails: Apply through the official scholarship link for complete submission instructions.

name: LaVerne Noyes Foundation Scholarship
description: Laverne Noyes Foundation Scholarships are renewable scholarships, awarded to a limited number of qualified students registered at The University of Texas at Austin, who are blood descendants of individuals who rendered service in World War I between April 6, 1917, and November 11, 1918 and whose service was terminated by death or honorable discharge. Blood descent does not result in an automatic award. Financial need will be used to distinguish among applicants. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. Current UT undergraduates and incoming external transfer undergraduates can apply through the OSFA Continuing & Transfer Student Scholarship Application, available from November through mid-March each year. All entering first-time undergraduate students are encouraged to submit the Scholarship Interest Form to be considered for the maximum number of scholarships from the Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid. The Scholarship Interest Form is available in LASSO each year from September 1 - January 15.

name: LBJ School of Public Affairs Admissions Fellowships
description: LBJ School applicants are automatically considered for merit-based and needs-based fellowships during the admissions review process as long as funding remains available. No additional application is required. If you receive an admissions fellowship, you will be notified with your admissions offer. There are three (3) types of admissions fellowship awarded: Stipends paid directly to the student Resident tuition awards paid directly to The University of Texas at Austin on behalf of the student awarded Non-resident tuition awards or waivers paid directly to The University of Texas at Austin on behalf of the student awarded that decrease the out-of-state portion of the tuition billed. If you have any questions, please contact the LBJ School's Office of Student Affairs and Admissions at lbjfellowships@austin.utexas.edu.

name: Leticia Flores Penn Endowed Presidential Scholarship in Piano
description: To support outstanding piano students in the Butler School of Music. Recipients must study performance or collaborative piano. To be awarded this Endowed Presidential Scholarship, students must be enrolled full-time in consecutive long sessions during the award period. Financial need may be considered. [Undergraduates Only] Undergraduate recipients must be a junior or senior classification with a minimum 3.25 GPA and at least 45 credit hours in residence at the university. [Graduate Students only] Graduate student recipients must hold at least one baccalaureate degree. Scholastic, artistic, musical and creative merit will be considered alongside leadership in university or industry settings. name: Liberal Arts Academic Excellence Award
description: The Liberal Arts Academic Excellence Award is a competitive scholarship program that matches top-ranked students with endowed scholarships offered through the College of Liberal Arts (COLA) Dean’s Office. Applications are reviewed by a committee, and awards are based on academic merit, financial need, a combination of both, or other specific criteria. Please note that awards are not guaranteed. Application Instructions Apply via the COLA Common Scholarship Application in the LASSO portal If eligible to apply, the application will appear in your LASSO account Application opens January 12, 2026 at 12:00 a.m. and closes March 1, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. Eligibility Requirements Currently enrolled Liberal Arts undergraduate at The University of Texas at Austin Minimum in-residence UT GPA of 3.25 At least 12 in-residence credit hours completed Students graduating May 2026 are not eligible to apply. Additional Information Scholarship funds are disbursed in the fall and spring of the following academic year Recipients must maintain full-time enrollment during both semesters of the award year For questions, contact the Liberal Arts Scholarships Office at cola-scholarships@austin.utexas.edu

At least one scholarship for a minimum of $500 will be awarded per year for students who have a disability that impairs their ability to access printed material. Scholarship is awarded in the fall semester.

name: Mahatma Gandhi Memorial Scholarship
description: In honor of Mahatma Gandhi, an annual scholarship of $500 will be awarded to two undergraduate students at The University of Texas at Austin based on academic excellence and dedication to the study of India. Award Recipient Requirements To be eligible a student must have taken a minimum of nine semester credit hours in courses relating directly to India and/or a minimum of two semesters of an Indian language. Award Recipient Preferences Students studying India and who have taken a minimum of nine semester credit hours in courses relating directly to India and/or a minimum of two semesters of an Indian language. Award Application Information Students must submit the application. An informal transcript of their undergraduate work. Two letters of recommendation from professors who are familiar with the applicant's work in the area of Indian Studies. (Recommendation letters can also be those used on the current FLAS application for SAI) Letters, transcripts, and application can be sent to sai@austin.utexas.edu Applications to apply open on Wednesday, November 1, 2023. Deadline to Apply: Friday, February 2, 2024

name: Margery Engel Loeb and Robert H. Loeb Graduate Fellowship in Social Work
description: Funds distributed shall be awarded to graduate students in the School of Social Work with a preference for students with demonstrated financial need. All awards made from this endowment shall be in accordance with the social work principle of social justice. Social justice, as explicated in the core values of the Steve Hicks School of Social Work, is a basic value and desired goal in democratic societies and includes equitable and fair access to societal institutions, laws, resources, and opportunities, right, goods, and services and services for all groups and individual without arbitrary limitations or barriers based on observed or interpretations of the value of differences in age, culture, physical or mental disability, education, income, language, national origin or religion. If you are an applicant or current social work student and would like to be considered for this or other scholarships administered by the UT School of Social Work, please complete the appropriate scholarship application (BSW or MSSW). name: Mark and Sarah Murray Scholarship for International Study - Fall 2026

Awardees will be selected based on application merit and financial need. Residency requirements: None Interview requirements: None Number of references required: None The application will be available February 1st - April 1st, 2026. To apply for this award, log in to LASSO or click the "Apply Now" button at the top of the Scholarships Search page. Find the Mark and Sarah Murray Scholarship for International Study - Fall 2026 application in the list of scholarships and click "Start Application" to the right of the page. Students should be prepared to answer essay questions and questions regarding their program. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee receiving an award.

Applications will be scored in consideration of the applicants' past experiences (including career and internships) and involvement on campus and in the community.
