name: Pan American Round Table (PART) of Austin Scholarship
description: The Pan American Round Table (PART) of Austin is an outside scholarship provider that offers three scholarship opportunities. 1. The PART Austin University of Texas at Austin Scholarship (initiated in honor of Charles Wilson and Jeannie Hunter Hackett) of $2,000 is awarded to an outstanding Latin American undergraduate (freshman, sophomore, junior, or senior) student (female or male) enrolled in the University of Texas at Austin. Enrollment must be current with the date of application. 2. PART Austin First Generation Scholarship (initiated by Gloria Mata Pennington) of $2,000 is awarded to a Latina undergraduate student only, (high school students or graduate students are not eligible), residing in the Greater Austin area, who has been accepted and is enrolled in a college or university in any year (freshman, sophomore, junior, or senior) of her undergraduate studies and who would be the first in her family to attend college and receive a degree. 3. PART Austin Post-Secondary Scholarship (initiated in honor of Mitty Guerra) of $2,000 is awarded to an economically disadvantaged Latina undergraduate student only (high school students or graduate students are not eligible), residing in the Greater Austin area, for the purpose of completing her educational goals. Eligibility requirements include enrollment in a trade or vocational program, community college, or four-year college or university. For more information and link to the application, visit: SCHOLARSHIPS – PART of Austin (austinpanam.com) 2024 – 2025 Scholarships open January 1, 2025 For questions about any of the scholarships, contact the Scholarship Chair at scholarship@austinpanam.com.
submissionDetails: Enrollment must be current with the date of application. name: Patricia L. Johnston Biological Sciences Student Research Fund
description: Scholarships to support activities of graduate or undergraduate students conducting research that may be relevant to timely disease or human health topics. Award may be used for purposes such as tuition, fees, books, internships, training, conferences, equipment and supplies, and professional travel and housing related to the student's research. Students must submit the CNS Common Scholarship Application available through LASSO to be considered. The CNS Common Scholarship Application typically opens to current students in early January with a March 15 deadline. Applicants interested in this award should complete the short essay question found on the CNS Common Scholarship Application pertaining to information about their research, and any anticipated financial needs such as tuition, fees, books, internships, training, conferences, equipment and supplies, and professional travel and housing related to the student's research. Contact scholarships@cns.utexas.edu with questions regarding the CNS Common Scholarship Application.

name: Patsy Stice Memorial Graduate Fellowship in Social Work
description: Funds distributed from this scholarship shall be used to support graduate students applying for admission to or already enrolled in the Steve Hicks School of Social Work and who plan to complete the clinical social work concentration. Applicants must be citizens or permanent residents of the United States of America with a cumulative GPA of 3.00. Applicants should have been out of the normal school cycle for a period of four years and then decided to return to college for graduate work. Fellowship shall be awarded for two academic semesters, during which time the fellowship recipient must maintain a GPA of 3.00. A student returning to college after several years absence may make less than a 3.00 GPA for his/her first semester. Applicants with financial need have priority. Applicants who are single parents have priority. If you are an applicant or current social work student and would like to be considered for this or other scholarships administered by the UT School of Social Work, please complete the appropriate scholarship application (BSW or MSSW). name: Paul L. Leventhal Fellowship
description: *For Current LBJ School Master's Students* The Paul L. Leventhal Fellowship is awarded annually on a competitive basis as part of the LBJ School's internship fellowship process and is made possible by the LBJ School's Nuclear Proliferation Prevention Project (NPPP). This fellowship honors the late Paul Leventhal who advocated tirelessly for the protection of the United States and the world from the proliferation of nuclear weapons and the materials and technology to make such weapons. Mr. Leventhal worked as an aide in the Senate from 1972 to 1981 and was instrumental in the passage of many landmark pieces of nuclear legislation. name: Phyllis and Thomas Leiser Endowed Excellence Fund in Undergraduate Energy Management
description: Funds distributed from the endowment to be used to support faculty, students, and programs in the energy management area of the McCombs School. Specifically, funds should be used to support development of an Undergraduate and Graduate Energy Management Program and to support the development of curriculum, programs, and activities in the energy management area. Should no such activities be available, funds should be used by the finance department at the McCombs School. Types and amounts of such uses determined by the dean in consultation with the director of the Energy Management and Innovation Center and may include, but are not limited to, the payment of salaries to faculty to support research, faculty development, curriculum development and course development; salary supplements to attract, retain and develop outstanding faculty; scholarships; student recruitment activities; energy management student organizations; industry conferences; alumni programs; honoraria; travel; and other operating expenses. Of these types of uses, the donors strongly prefer that funds be used for undergraduate scholarships and that preference for these scholarships be given to students studying energy management who are members of Sigma Phi Lambda or Beta Upsilon Chi, as long as these organizations remain in good standing and are officially registered with the Office of the Dean of Students, or a successor office.

name: Randy Diehl Prize in Liberal Arts
description: Merit-based prize awarded annually to a graduating College of Liberal Arts senior who has indicated that they will commit the year after graduation to service for the greater good. The intent behind this award is to encourage recently graduated Liberal Arts majors to use their considerable skills in communication and in understanding cultures, histories, philosophies, and literature to affect positive change in the world. This change can achieved through service with a non-profit, a for-profit, a city organization, or through a leadership role in creating a non-profit that will benefit society. One $18,000 award is made per academic year. Eligibility Requirements: Liberal Arts students graduating in May of 2025 Demonstrated plans to commit the 25/26 year after graduation to service with a non-profit, a for-profit, a city organization, or through a leadership role in creating a non-profit that will benefit society. IMPORTANT: Recipients must be enrolled full-time at UT during the semester they apply. One award winner will be selected by a College Scholarship Committee and announced by the end of the spring semester. Scholarship funds will be released once degree is conferred. A brief report describing your experience six months to one year after graduation will be required. Application will be available in LASSO mid-February 2025.
submissionDetails: IMPORTANT: Recipients must be enrolled full-time at UT during the semester they apply. Application will be available in LASSO mid-February 2025.

name: Randy Kelleher Endowed Scholarship
description: Awarded to undergraduate students in the Department of Radio-Television-Film with a minimum of 60 hours undergraduate credit and 3.0 GPA. submissionDetails: Apply through the official scholarship link for complete submission instructions.

name: Rapoport-King Thesis Scholarship
description: The Rapoport-King Thesis Scholarships honor Audre and Bernard Rapoport and Robert D. King, former Dean of the College of Liberal Arts. Audre and Bernard Rapoport of Waco, Texas have provided an endowment that enables the College of Liberal Arts to provide scholarship and research support for those students who are writing a thesis in one of the Departmental Honors Programs the year they apply. To be eligible for a Rapoport-King Scholarship, candidates must be enrolled as a Plan I student and must be planning to write a senior thesis in one of the College's departmental honors programs. They must be working on the thesis as part of a Fall - Spring sequence. Fellows will be chosen on basis of academic record, the quality of the thesis proposal, and financial need. As Mr. Rapoport has put it: the candidates should be ""smart and deserving."" The dean will make final recommendations. Those chosen will be awarded a $3,000 scholarship for the senior year only; it will be awarded in two payments over two consecutive long semesters. In addition, faculty members will be provided with a $1,000 research stipend, and it is expected that the student and mentor will work closely together. The Rapoport-King Thesis Scholarship application requires a thesis proposal, statement of financial need, and a letter of recommendation from your thesis supervisor
submissionDetails: King, former Dean of the College of Liberal Arts. name: RGK Nonprofit Internship Fund

name: Samuel George Cook Memorial Endowed Presidential Scholarship
description: Income to fund annually awarded scholarships for students who will be enrolled in the McCombs School of Business as Freshmen. Recipient must be a full-time student (12 or more semester hours), enrolled as a Freshman (incoming or continuing) with a major in Business Administration. A recipient may receive an award payment for the semester during which he or she will earn more than 30 semester credit hours of total college credit, but may not receive an award for any subsequent semester. Recipient must be designated as a Texas Resident at the time of admission to UT Austin. Must have satisfactory (average or above) academic record. For first-time entering Freshmen and Freshmen transfer applicants, eligibility for admission to the College of Business Administration will be taken as constituting a satisfactory academic record. For current UT Austin Freshman applicants, a minimum 2.5 GPA will be required to constitute a satisfactory academic record.

name: Sarah (Bunny) and Leo Horvitz Memorial Scholarship - Fall 2026
description: The Sarah (Bunny) and Leo Horvitz Memorial Scholarship is open to undergraduate students studying abroad in Israel, students researching, interning or studying in the region, and/or who are completing a virtual exchange program through an institutional partner in Israel. Locations: Israel, Bahrain, Iran, Iraq, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Oman, Palestine, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Syria, United Arab Emirates, Yemen Minimum in-residence GPA required: 2.75 The application will be available from February 1st, 2026 until April 1st, 2026. To apply for this award, log in to LASSO or click the "Apply Now" button at the top of the Scholarships Search page. Find the Sarah (Bunny) and Leo Horvitz Memorial Scholarship - Fall 2026 application in the list of scholarships and click "Start Application" to the right of the page. Students should be prepared to answer essay questions and questions regarding their program. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee receiving an award.

name: Society of Iranian-American Women for Education Scholarship
description: The Society of Iranian-American Women for Education (SIAWE) Scholarship is a $3,500 spring award that recognizes a student who has lived or matriculated from Iran or has been a dependent of individuals who have lived in Iran for the majority of their lives, demonstrates financial need, and academic excellence. In addition to the online application, the following criteria must be met: You must be registered as a full-time student at UT Austin during the semester in which the scholarship is awarded. Have lived or matriculated from Iran or have been a dependent of individuals who have lived in Iran for the majority of their lives. You must have completed a full course of study for two academic semesters at UT Austin. Have a minimum GPA of 3.0 for undergraduates or 3.75 for graduate students and maintain the minimum GPA for the duration of the award. Demonstrate financial need. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee receiving an award.
submissionDetails: In addition to the online application, the following criteria must be met: You must be registered as a full-time student at UT Austin during the semester in which the scholarship is awarded.

(3,500 character max)

name: Subiendo Endowed Scholarship for Rising Leaders
description: Funds shall provide full Texas resident undergraduate tuition, plus room/board stipend to include books, living expenses, and enrichment activities, including but not limited to, study abroad opportunities and internships. Recipients must be enrolled full time at McCombs (unless in their last semester and a less than full time course load will enable them to complete their degree) and have attended Subiendo: The Academy for Rising Leaders program; be U.S. citizens/permanent residents, Texas residents, and have demonstrated financial need. Academic achievement may be considered. Recipients must maintain minimum GPA required by McCombs. Awards should be made to incoming freshmen; they have opportunity to receive award for up to 4 years if they remain eligible.
submissionDetails: Apply through the official scholarship link for complete submission instructions.

name: Sue Surbey Shoemake Endowed Scholarship
description: To provide scholarship support for undergraduate, graduate and doctoral students in the McCombs School. Recipients must be enrolled full-time in the McCombs School of Business. Exceptions to this requirement may be made for students in their final semester prior to graduation if a less than a full course load is needed for graduation. Selection shall be primarily on academic merit, though financial need may be considered. At the discretion of the scholarship committee, financial need may outweigh applicants with higher academic merit. The scholarship may be renewed in successive years if applicant satisfies minimum requirements and reapplies.

name: Sylvia Shapiro Scholarship
description: Scholarships for students in the Steve Hicks School of Social at either the undergraduate or graduate level. Recipient shall be a full time student in the School of Social Work who is participating in a field practicum or internship relating to the frail elderly. Recipient shall receive the stipend at the beginning of the semester in which the practicum or internship is to be served, and may be awarded during the summer term. Criteria of selection shall be financial need, past academic performance, desire for service in the field of gerontology, potential for future contributions in the field, and a firm concept of career goals. Scholarship may be renewed for the second semester. If you are an applicant or current social work student and would like to be considered for this or other scholarships administered by the UT School of Social Work, please complete the appropriate scholarship application (BSW or MSSW). The 2026–2027 BSW Scholarship Application will open in February—don’t miss it! Watch your inbox for an email from the BSW Program with a direct link to apply. name: Terrell Blodgett Fellowship for Government Services in Urban Management and Finance
description: *For Current LBJ School Master's Students* The Blodgett Fellowship is awarded annually on a competitive basis as part of the LBJ School's internship fellowship process. First-year LBJ master's students pursuing their required internship in local government and/or city management are eligible to apply. The dollar amount of the award is $4,500 to be disbursed during the summer 2025 to support a summer internship and another $4,500 to be disbursed during the fall 2025 to support the student's second year of studies. The internship may be paid or unpaid. The fellowship also provides up to $3,000* to support attendance at national and state management association meetings geared towards local government: The Texas City Management Association (TCMA) annual conference is scheduled to be held in Arlington, TX June 4-7, 2026 The International City / County Management Association (ICMA) annual conference is scheduled to be held in Los Angeles County, CA October 17-21, 2026 *The amount of the conference funding is not a set amount ñ registration fees, airfare, and transportation will be based on invoices / receipts submitted; meals will be based on federal per diem rates. It will be the total of those expenses, up to a maximum of $3,000. You can submit receipts separately, for example: registration fees and airfare before you travel, then lodging and transportation after you travel.

name: Terry Foundation Scholarship
description: The Terry Foundation was established in 1986 by Houstonians Howard Terry (1916-2012) and Nancy Terry (1927-2012) out of a desire to help young people to help themselves. The Foundation’s goal is to strengthen the state of Texas by identifying Texas high school graduates who have promise of future leadership distinction and assisting them in developing their future and the futures of those around them. Who can apply? The Terry Foundation provides four-year scholarships for Texas resident students attending UT Austin and several other Universities in the state of Texas. Terry Scholars are selected from a highly competitive applicant pool of incoming freshmen who demonstrate exceptional leadership, character, and scholastic ability. Family income and financial need are also considered. Requirements: To be considered for the Terry Foundation Scholarship at The University of Texas at Austin you must: Complete your application for admission. Complete the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA). The FAFSA application will determine your eligibility for federal grants and is required for all Terry Foundation Candidates and Finalists. Complete the OSFA - Scholarship Interest Form which is available to Longhorn freshmen via LASSO or My Status from September 1 - January 15 each year.
submissionDetails: Who can apply? Requirements: To be considered for the Terry Foundation Scholarship at The University of Texas at Austin you must: Complete your application for admission. Complete the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA).

name: Texas Blazers Endowed Scholarship
description: This scholarship should be awarded to a Eastside Memorial High School graduate of any college or major. (1) If no applicant in a given semester is a graduate of Eastside Memorial High School, any graduating student from the allowed zip codes (78702, 78721, 78722, and 78723) should be eligible. (2.) Scholarship should be given to entering freshmen. If no suitable candidate is found among entering freshman, upperclassmen may apply. (3.) Financial need should be considered and should be weighed equally with academic performance. (4.) Factors to consider should include, but not be limited to, class rank, high school GPA, and standardized test scores. No specific criteria must be met for entering freshmen. Eligible upperclassmen must have a 3.0 GPA. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. All entering first-time undergraduate students are encouraged to submit the Scholarship Interest Form to be considered for the maximum number of scholarships from the Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid. The Scholarship Interest Form is available in LASSO each year from September 1 - January 15.

name: The Blair and Bubba Moffett Summer Entrepreneurship Academy (SEA) Scholarship
description: The SEA Scholarship is a need-based award designed to make entrepreneurship education more accessible and inclusive for all UT students participating in the Blair and Bubba Moffett Summer Entrepreneurship Academy. Offered through the Harkey Institute for Entrepreneurial Studies, this scholarship prioritizes students who demonstrate financial need and a strong interest in entrepreneurship, innovation, and leadership. Award Amounts range from $550 - $1550 and will be distributed among selected recipients. Students should be prepared to answer essay questions. Please note that meeting the scholarship criteria itself does not guarantee an award. Eligibility: Must be a current undergraduate student at UT Austin Must be accepted into the Summer Entrepreneurship Academy Must demonstrate financial need through the UT Austin LASSO portal Open to students from all majors and backgrounds Apply Now and take your first step toward building something bold.

name: The Herbert and Johanna Liebscher Foundation Endowed Scholarship Fund
description: To provide scholarships for undergraduate, graduate and doctoral students who have graduated from a public, private, or home school located in Comal County, Texas. If graduating from a home school, the recipient must have resided in Comal County, Texas, at the time of graduation. Students must be enrolled in the McCombs school of Business, with first preference given to declared Finance or Accounting majors, and second preference given to any Business major. Recipients must be enrolled full-time, except in their final semester prior to graduation if a less-than full-time course load is needed to complete their degree requirements. Recipients will be selected primarily on academic merit. Undergraduate and MBA students must maintain a minimum grade point average of 3.0. Freshmen may also be considered with demonstrated academic achievement and leadership in high school. Financial need will also be a consideration. Recipients with great financial need may be ranked above other students with somewhat better academic credentials. The scholarship may be awarded to the same student in successive years provided that the student satisfies the above minimum scholarship criteria and reapplies for the scholarship.

description: Funds distributed from the endowment shall be used to provide scholarships to undergraduate students in the Steve Hicks School of Social Work. The 2026–2027 BSW Scholarship Application will open in February—don’t miss it! Watch your inbox for an email from the BSW Program with a direct link to apply. The MSSW application will open December 1, 2025, and close February 1, 2026. Please find the MSSW application here. Deadline for Merit-Based Scholarships: January 5, 2026 Deadline for Need-Based and Professional Interest Scholarships: February 1, 2026 If you are a prospective Social Work PhD student, please contact Graduate Program Coordinator Abbey Morales at ssw-doctoral@austin.utexas.edu with questions regarding the PhD scholarship awarding process.

name: Truitt Family Graduate Fellowship in Russian, East European, and Eurasian Studies
description: The Truitt Family Graduate Fellowship in Russian, East European, and Eurasian Studies will provide a stipend of at least $1000 to incoming or continuing students of the CREEES MA Program. (The award may also include a non-resident tuition exemption waiver for out of state students.) Deadline: January 26, 2026 Eligibility and Criteria: All applicants to (and current students in) CREEES’ graduate programs and dual degrees are eligible for the Truitt Family Graduate Fellowship. Awardees will be selected based on criteria which include academic merit, research ambitions, proposed trajectory in the field, and financial need. Incoming Students: All CREEES MA applicants will be automatically considered for the fellowship based on submitted application materials. Only those who accept and enroll in the program are eligible to receive the Truitt award, which will be paid out in early fall semester in the year it is awarded. Continuing Students: Only currently enrolled students who are continuing in the program in the next academic year are eligible to apply for and receive this fellowship. These applicants will be required to fill out application and submit a transcript and one page statement on progress in the program and academic goals, and will receive funds in early summer of the year they are awarded. Please reach out to Catherine Forrest at catherine.forrest@austin.utexas.edu or creees@austin.utexas.edu with any questions.
submissionDetails: (The award may also include a non-resident tuition exemption waiver for out of state students.) name: Tu-Ting and Rachel Tsan Endowed Presidential Scholarship in Social Work
description: Funds distributed from this endowment shall be used to provide scholarship support for undergraduate students enrolled majoring in Social Work. Recipients must have at least 45 credit hours in residence at UT Austin and a cumulative GPA of at least 3.75. Recipients must be enrolled full-time. Exceptions to this requirement may be made for students if a less-than-full-time course load will enable students to complete their degree requirements. Strong preference should be given for juniors or seniors on track to graduate within four years or to complete a longer-than-four-year program within recommended program length. Preference should be given to students with financial need. Award is renewable if minimum requirements are met and applicant reapplies. If you are an applicant or current social work student and would like to be considered for this or other scholarships administered by the UT School of Social Work, please complete the appropriate scholarship application (BSW or MSSW). The 2026–2027 BSW Scholarship Application will open in February—don’t miss it! Watch your inbox for an email from the BSW Program with a direct link to apply. The MSSW application will open December 1, 2025, and close February 1, 2026. Please find the MSSW application here. Deadline for Merit-Based Scholarships: January 5, 2026 Deadline for Need-Based and Professional Interest Scholarships: February 1, 2026 If you are a prospective Social Work PhD student, please contact Graduate Program Coordinator Abbey Morales at ssw-doctoral@austin.utexas.edu with questions regarding the PhD scholarship awarding process.
submissionDetails: If you are an applicant or current social work student and would like to be considered for this or other scholarships administered by the UT School of Social Work, please complete the appropriate scholarship application (BSW or MSSW). The 2026–2027 BSW Scholarship Application will open in February—don’t miss it! Watch your inbox for an email from the BSW Program with a direct link to apply.

name: Tuition Assistance for Mexican Students (TAMS) - Spring 2026 & Summer 2026
description: The Tuition Assistance for Mexican Students (TAMS) provides a waiver of the nonresident portion of a student's tuition bill. The TAMS is not a cash award. To be eligible for the TAMS award, you must be a citizen and resident of Mexico who demonstrates financial need. Receiving the TAMS award is not guaranteed as the application is competitive due to the limited number of awards. Additionally, the TAMS can be renewed annually provided that the recipient submits a renewal application, maintains eligibility, and graduates in a timely manner. Renewal of the TAMS award is not guaranteed and is dependent on both the amount of awards available and continued demonstration of financial need. TAMS is administered by Texas Global as required by the Texas Education Code and Administrative Code. Additional information can be found here. TAMS is only for formula-funded courses (Option I) and Option III students are not eligible for the waiver. Additional information about Option III can be found here. To apply for this award, log in to LASSO or click the "Apply Now" button at the top of the Scholarships Search page. Find the Tuition Assistance for Mexican Students (TAMS) - Spring 2026 & Summer 2026 application in the list of scholarships and click "Start Application" to the right of the page. Please note that meeting the scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding.
submissionDetails: Receiving the TAMS award is not guaranteed as the application is competitive due to the limited number of awards. name: UT Engineering Scholarship Application for Current Students
description: Please complete the ENTIRE application to be considered for 2026-2027 Cockrell School of Engineering scholarships. The more information you provide, the better your department can match you with possible scholarships, using lots of different criteria, some including: * Merit and/or financial need * Focusing on certain technical areas, engineering majors * Graduating from specific regions or high schools * Working while in school or participating in research * Participating in student organizations, campus activities or volunteering And, SO MUCH MORE! This application is based on your college career. If you’re a first-year student, please limit high school activities if you choose to include a few. Click Add Answer (multiple times) to enter your participation in organizations and activities. There’s a section at the end for Special Considerations if you would like to give information about a personal situation or unique details you feel should be considered when reviewing your application. The goal of this application is to provide the best summary of your activities and financial situation. It’s not the FAFSA, so just use your best estimate to enter information. When the financial section asks about “next year” it refers to 2026-2027. You should be able to start the application and come back to it if you need to complete and submit it later. If you were awarded a multi-year (4 year) scholarship when you were admitted to UT, you DO NOT need to complete the application.

Current UT undergraduates and incoming external transfer undergraduates can apply through the OSFA Continuing & Transfer Student Scholarship Application, available from November through mid-March each year. All entering first-time undergraduate students are encouraged to submit the Scholarship Interest Form to be considered for the maximum number of scholarships from the Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid. The Scholarship Interest Form is available in LASSO each year from September 1 - January 15.

name: Voyager Scholarship
description: The Voyager Scholarship Program is a two-year, leadership development and scholarship program. Through a transformational combination of barrier-reducing financial aid, an immersive work-travel experience, and a robust network of leaders and mentors, the Voyager Scholarship is designed to inspire, empower, and connect the next generation of leaders and equip them with the tools and resources they need to launch their careers in public service. The program identifies students in their third year of undergraduate studies who have a passion for public service. Over the course of two years, programming will facilitate opportunities for students to refine their values-based leadership approach, build bridges across communities, and nurture their sense of curiosity as they deepen their commitment to pursuing public service careers.

name: Wagner Scholarship
description: To award scholarships to one or more deserving undergraduate students who meet the following guidelines. 1. Is a child of an active officer of the Fort Worth, Texas Police Department who is in good standing with the Department, or the child of a retired, deceased, or disabled officer of the Department who was in good standing at the time of their departure. 2. Is enrolled full-time at UT, per semester. 3. Is in good academic standing; HOWEVER, ACADEMIC MERIT SHOULD NOT OTHERWISE BE AN ELIMINATING FACTOR. 4. Has proven success toward the timely pursuit of their degree requirements-such as, has successfully completed and passed a full-time equivalent of coursework in the semester prior to applying for scholarship (not applicable to first semester Freshmen applicants) 5. This scholarship may be awarded to the same student in consecutive years, provided that the student reapplies and continues to meet the stated guidelines. 6. In the case where two or more students equally qualify on the basis of the above criteria, preference should be given to the student who has a demonstrated financial need. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. Current UT undergraduates and incoming external transfer undergraduates can apply through the OSFA Continuing & Transfer Student Scholarship Application, available from November through mid-March each year. All entering first-time undergraduate students are encouraged to submit the Scholarship Interest Form to be considered for the maximum number of scholarships from the Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid. The Scholarship Interest Form is available in LASSO each year from September 1 - January 15.

name: Ward C. Mayborn Journalism Scholarship
description: Criteria: -Journalism major -Junior or Senior -Intend to become a newspaper man or woman -Should show initiative in supporting him- or herself -Financial need (determined by FAFSA information) If you would like to be considered for this award, please provide a written statement describing how you have shown initiative in supporting yourself. (3,500 character max)
submissionDetails: Criteria: -Journalism major -Junior or Senior -Intend to become a newspaper man or woman -Should show initiative in supporting him- or herself -Financial need (determined by FAFSA information) If you would like to be considered for this award, please provide a written statement describing how you have shown initiative in supporting yourself.

name: Ward Creative Communications, Inc. Scholarship
description: Undergraduate scholarship for students enrolled in the Moody College of Communication who have at least a 3.0 GPA and who have financial need. Students should answer the following question as part of the scholarship application: Authenticity is a prerequisite to effective communication. Communications professionals often are perceived as spin-doctors - the antithesis of authentic communication. Give examples from recent or significant news when authenticity was clearly missing. How did the event unfold? What could have been different if communication had been more authentic between the parties involved and media? How would authenticity improve the world?
submissionDetails: Students should answer the following question as part of the scholarship application: Authenticity is a prerequisite to effective communication.

name: Warren Skaaren Endowed Presidential Scholarship
description: Criteria: -Radio-Television-Film majors -Junior, Senior, Graduate Students -Financial need (as determined by FAFSA) -Undergraduate students: minimum 3.25 GPA and 45 hours in residence at UT -Graduate students must hold at least one baccalaureate degree; scholastic merit should include artistic, musical and creative merit, and leadership roles in current or prior university or industry settings may be considered -Recipient must enroll full-time at UT Austin during the award period -Leadership roles and financial need may be considered by the committee. -Pursuing a major in the film industry field (including screenwriting, directing, and cinematography) If you would like to be considered for this award, please provide a link (to Vimeo, YouTube, Google Docs, UTBox, etc.) where committee members may view a work sample. This sample must be either a complete screenplay with synopsis or a completed film project.
submissionDetails: Apply through the official scholarship link for complete submission instructions.

name: Wayne Abramson Endowed Presidential Scholarship
description: Scholarship open to undergraduates from any field of study. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. This scholarship is available through the OSFA Endowed Presidential Scholarship Application, available to eligible students in LASSO each spring semester.
submissionDetails: This scholarship is available through the OSFA Endowed Presidential Scholarship Application, available to eligible students in LASSO each spring semester.

name: Weaver Endowed Scholarship in Accounting
description: For graduate students enrolled in the Master in Professional Accounting (MPA) program in the McCombs School of Business. Recipients must be enrolled full-time in the McCombs School of Business, unless in their final semester a less-than-full-time course load will enable them to complete degree requirements. Students in the integrated MPA and MPA Bridge programs will receive an invitation to apply for scholarships before entering graduate school. Students in the traditional MPA program are automatically considered for a potential scholarship after gaining admission (a scholarship application is not required).

name: Will Rogers Memorial Scholarship
description: Income to be used for the education, training, or other assistance of handicapped students or students preparing themselves for work with handicapped children. The University is free to determine the amount of the scholarship to aid pupils who could not otherwise prepare themselves for this work. Can be used to help handicapped students prepare for admission to the University. By the use of the term handicapped, it is intended to include students handicapped physically or mentally, or handicapped by reason of environment or lack of opportunity, or such related causes, and thereby incapacitated to take advantage of the opportunities that the Government of the United States holds open to all of its citizens. Meeting scholarship eligibility does not guarantee awarding. Current UT undergraduates and incoming external transfer undergraduates can apply through the OSFA Continuing & Transfer Student Scholarship Application, available from November through mid-March each year. All entering first-time undergraduate students are encouraged to submit the Scholarship Interest Form to be considered for the maximum number of scholarships from the Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid. The Scholarship Interest Form is available in LASSO each year from September 1 - January 15.

name: William & Judith Bollinger International Student Enrichment Fund
description: *For Current LBJ School International Master's Students* The William and Judith Bollinger International Student Enrichment Fund was established to enable international students studying at the LBJ School to pursue educational and cultural interests, within the United States, both at and away from The University of Texas at Austin. The funds are meant to elevate the experiences of international students by expanding their knowledge and inspiring them to share that knowledge with their peers, friends, and family back home. Individual international master's students at the LBJ School can receive up to $2,500 annually until the funds are exhausted. For more information about this award please visit the LBJ School's Fellowships for Current Students webpage.
